derived_constructs.licensing_note: Introduces two-valued value/status distinction required by CRE-001 model; not primitive-only.
Introduces order preservation for history; licensed only with a primitive that supports ordering, history points, or history structure.
Introduces guarded transition execution; not arbitrary programming language.
Introduces OR guard for halt; limited to registered finite disjunction.
Introduces terminal blocking policy and output treatment under registered ambiguity choices.
Historical compiler-local compatibility heuristic; superseded for semantic licensing by these frozen semantics.
Replay evidence for deterministic implementation; not primitive semantics.
derived_constructs.meaning: native construct to common-model trace links

derived_constructs.licensing_note: Frozen assumptions for CRE-001 deterministic scope; stronger claims require separate justification.
derived_constructs.meaning: disable/reject repeatability, prohibited transition output, unterminated output

derived_constructs.licensing_note: No formal semantics frozen here beyond recording non-support in CRE-001 deterministic scope.

scope: official CRE-001 vocabulary primitives and declared derived machinery only
status: Frozen vocabulary semantics for CRE-001 scope

compiler interpretation: Transformation as transition schema and update carrier
lowering rules: atomic variables, guards, updates, terminal condition, outputs, invariants, ambiguity policies
primitive definitions: transformations may express transition/update/rule application/state modification

closure_conditions: closed under finite CRE-001-scoped composition when every component remains explicit and no forbidden commitment is introduced
equivalence_conditions: equivalent only when identifier, role, scope, operational contribution, and licensed commitments match
examples: Object used with recorded role in CRE-001 mapping
primitive_semantics.formal_definition: Within CRE-001 scope, Object denotes a discrete item, entity, token, state-bearing item, rule, or record treated as a unit of representation. It licenses only explicit, finitely identified uses satisfying its source definition and recorded role.
identity_conditions: object identity is by declared identifier within CRE-001 mapping scope
interaction_rules: may interact with other primitives only through explicit derived machinery or recorded mapping relations
does not silently import another vocabulary primitive
limitations: formalized only for CRE-001 vocabulary-semantics scope
does not license stronger project-level conclusions
non_examples: Object used to import an unrecorded domain rule or universal conclusion
optional_properties: link to derived construct when used
permitted_commitments: unit individuation
explicit CRE-001-scoped representation when provenance and role are recorded
primitive_semantics.source_definition: a discrete item, entity, token, state-bearing item, rule, or record treated as a unit of representation.

limitations: formalized only for CRE-001 vocabulary-semantics scope
does not license stronger project-level conclusions

optional_properties: link to derived construct when used
permitted_commitments: explicit typed connection among Objects
explicit CRE-001-scoped representation when provenance and role are recorded

compiler interpretation: Label carries most atomic values and conditions
State supports configuration semantics through labeled assignments
implementation assumptions: transition effects are deterministic under registered policy
lowering rules: labels lower to variables/guards/effects/outputs when their attachment role is explicit
primitive definitions: state as assignment/status/record/condition at discrete history point
label as name/marker/status/condition/identifier attached to state/transition/item
